channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: American small business owners fight to survive crippling dine-and-dash crime sprees
item.description: Restaurant industry experts are sounding the alarm as dine-and-dash incidents gain national attention, threatening the bottom lines of small business owners throughout the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A US-Russian crew of 3 launches to the International Space Station
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — NASA astronaut Jonny Kim and two Russian crewmates launched successfully to the International Space Station on Tuesday on board a Russian spacecraft.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky, residents closely monitored waterways for signs they had crested, but freezing temperatures forecast for Tuesday could complicate any cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit on trial for torture in US
item.description: Opening statements are expected Tuesday in the U.S. trial of a man accused of torturing political opponents of Gambia’s ex-military dictator Yahya Jammeh nearly 20 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Slevin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Clem Burke, multifaceted drummer of iconic rock group Blondie, has died
item.description: Clem Burke, whose versatile drumming propelled the iconic rock group Blondie during its decades of music that spanned from new-wave punk to disco-infused tunes, has died.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Philadelphia police draw on personal experiences to respond to mental health crises
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — On a frigid February morning, Vanity Cordero, a Philadelphia police officer, heard a call over the radio for a man threatening to jump from a bridge. The details sounded familiar.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Swollen rivers are flooding towns in the US South after a prolonged deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky on Monday, submerging neighborhoods and threatening a famed bourbon distillery in the state capital.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Young NYC woman stabbed in neck with glass shard while strolling around ritzy shopping hub
item.description: A crazed New York City man allegedly stabbed a woman in the neck with a shard of glass while she was walking around SoHo, a ritzy shopping, on Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee repeat felon charged in Rhodes College student's 2021 murder learns his fate
item.description: Memphis, Tennessee, felon Rainess Holmes has been sentenced in connection with Rhodes College student Andrew Rainer's October 2021 murder during a home invasion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over death of her husband
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Lori Vallow Daybell, who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again Monday. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over husband's death
item.description: Lori Vallow Daybell, the Idaho mother with doomsday religious beliefs who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Catholic bishops end refugee aid partnerships with US government, citing funding cuts
item.description: The U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops announced Monday that it is ending a half-century of partnerships with the federal government to serve refugees and migrant children, saying the “heartbreaking” decision follows the Trump administration’s abrupt halt to funding.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: Step inside Universal's 'Epic' new theme park
item.description: Universal Orlando has revealed the first details of its highly anticipated new theme park, Epic Universe, which will officially open on May 22.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Jay North, TV's mischievous Dennis the Menace, dies at 73
item.description: LAKE BUTLER, Fla. (AP) — Jay North, who starred as the towheaded mischief maker on TV's “Dennis the Menace” for four seasons starting in 1959, has died. He was 73.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Maine sues the Trump administration over funding freeze after dispute over transgender athletes
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — Maine officials sued the administration of President Donald Trump on Monday to try to stop the government from freezing federal money in the wake of a dispute over transgender athletes in sports.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to block return of man wrongly deported to El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday asked the Supreme Court to block a court order requiring it to return a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador. The Justice Department argued in an emergency appeal to the justices that U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Mega Millions tickets rise to $5, but the lottery promises more giant jackpots
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Mega Millions players will get slightly better odds and should start seeing more billion-dollar jackpots, but at a cost — literally — with tickets for the multistate lottery jumping in price to $5.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Stocks sink globally as Trump pushes ahead with trade war
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much financial markets would love to see him do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Miami’s ‘Little Venezuela’ fears Trump's moves against migration
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Wilmer Escaray left Venezuela in 2007 and enrolled at Miami Dade College, opening his first restaurant six years later.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariffs pose danger for vulnerable Republicans
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are being put on defense amid the fallout from President Donald Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked uncertainty about the economy.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jay North, TV's Dennis the Menace, dies at 73
item.description: North was 6 when he was cast as the smiling troublemaker in the CBS sitcom adaptation of Hank Ketcham's popular comic strip that took place in an idyllic American suburb.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ mom who drowned her 2 kids to root out 'all the evil in the world' found not guilty by reason of insanity
item.description: A New Jersey woman who drowned her toddlers last year for "religious reasons" was found not guilty by reason of insanity and sentenced to a secure psychiatric hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what to know about nations considering the 1st global tax on emissions for shipping
item.description: Nations are trying to reach an agreement to charge commercial vessels a fee for their emissions in what would effectively be the world’s first global carbon tax.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justin Turner's error allows Fernando Tatis Jr. to score go-ahead run in Padres' 8-7 win over Cubs
item.description: Luis Arraez had four hits, Fernando Tatis Jr. scored the go-ahead run in the ninth inning when first baseman Justin Turner couldn't make the catch to finish a double play, and the San Diego Padres beat the Chicago Cubs 8-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TROTTO Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Second child died from measles-related causes in West Texas
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Lithgow wins best actor at the UK stage Olivier Awards for exploring Roald Dahl's dark side
item.description: American actor John Lithgow won the best actor trophy at the London stage Olivier Awards on Sunday for exploring the dark side of children's writer Roald Dahl in “Giant.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge calls Trump's deportation of Maryland man 'wholly lawless' in scathing decision
item.description: A federal judge, in a scathing decision on Sunday, said the Trump administration had no legal grounds to arrest, detain, and deport a Salvadoran national living in Maryland to a prison in his home country, saying the decision was “wholly lawless.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, ABC 4

item.title: 22,600 lbs of bratwurst recalled in 10 states over possible plastic contamination
item.description: Federal health officials say thousands of pounds of bratwurst sold in 10 states are being recalled because the sausages may contain hard plastic.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street braces for opening bell Monday, concerns over recession grow in wake of President Trump's new tariffs
item.description: Financial markets last week had their worst week since the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic in the wake of President Donald Trump's announcement of sweeping new tariffs. Foreign trade partners have already retaliated, and now concerns over a recession are growing among financial experts.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Halligan, WGN 9
